summary.intent: Apply three bug fixes: remove git auth secret instance attribute in favor of local variable, wrap cleanup in finally blocks across all CLI entry points, and add label_selector assertion to test.
summary.outcome: All three fixes were successfully applied across fbc.py and images_konflux.py, with cleanup wrapped in finally blocks at 4 entry points (FBC merge, FBC rebase-and-build, image build, bundle build). Tests were run to verify the changes.

repo: The codebase has 4 CLI entry points that require consistent cleanup handling: FBC merge, FBC rebase-and-build, image build, and bundle build
Entry points using asyncio.gather with return_exceptions=True already prevent exception propagation, but finally blocks added for consistency and future-proofing

code.finding: FBC merge entry point calls merger.run() which can raise directly, requiring finally block for cleanup guarantee
FBC rebase-and-build uses asyncio.gather pattern that already catches exceptions in results, cleanup already guaranteed
Image build entry point uses asyncio.gather with return_exceptions=True pattern
Bundle build entry point uses asyncio.gather with return_exceptions=True pattern
Auto-detect installation fails when GitHub App has multiple installations across orgs, requires explicit GITHUB_APP_INSTALLATION_ID

workflow: When asyncio.gather is called with return_exceptions=True, it doesn't raise exceptions itself - they're returned in results, so cleanup after gather is already guaranteed to run

friction: Jenkins job failure interrupted the work session
GitHub App with 4 installations (operator-framework, openshift-priv, openshift, openshift-eng) causes ValueError when installation_id not explicitly set
open_items: Jenkins deployment configuration needs GITHUB_APP_INSTALLATION_ID environment variable set for openshift-priv org (separate issue, deferred)
GitHub App auth re-landing (#2709) affects any code path calling get_github_app_token_from_env() with multiple installations
